pattern: westus2 hosts 3,201 of 9,707 regional resources (33.0%), followed by eastus (2,117 / 21.8%), centralindia (1,134 / 11.7%), and southeastasia (1,083 / 11.2%); the remaining ~22% is spread across 11+ smaller regions.
implication: Default new regional workloads to westus2 (primary) with eastus as the secondary/paired region, and only fall back to centralindia or southeastasia when geographic proximity to those user bases is required.

pattern: 96.1% (563 of 585) of resources reporting properties.minimumTlsVersion are pinned to TLS 1.2 (TLS1_2 or 1.2); only 3.9% still report TLS 1.0.
implication: Set minimumTlsVersion to TLS1_2 on every TLS-capable resource generated in the plan to match the tenant's established TLS floor.

pattern: 89.4% (1,167 of 1,306) of resources reporting properties.publicNetworkAccess have it Enabled; only 10.0% are Disabled and 0.5% are SecuredByPerimeter.
implication: Default publicNetworkAccess to Enabled for new resources unless the workload description explicitly calls for private-only access; do not assume a private-endpoint-only posture.

pattern: Storage accounts overwhelmingly enforce secure-transport and block anonymous blob access: supportsHttpsTrafficOnly is True on 460 of 464 accounts (99.1%) and allowBlobPublicAccess is False on 463 of 464 accounts (99.8%).
implication: Provision new storage accounts with supportsHttpsTrafficOnly=true and allowBlobPublicAccess=false by default to match the tenant's storage hardening baseline.

pattern: 81.3% (279 of 343) of storage accounts reporting properties.allowSharedKeyAccess have it disabled (False), and 91.5% (292 of 319) have allowCrossTenantReplication disabled.
implication: Disable shared-key access and cross-tenant replication on new storage accounts and rely on Entra ID / managed-identity auth, in line with the tenant's storage convention.

pattern: Storage redundancy SKUs lean local-only: Standard_LRS accounts for 233 of 419 (55.6%) of storage SKUs, with Standard_GRS (24.6%) and Standard_RAGRS (19.8%) making up the rest.
implication: Default new general-purpose storage accounts to Standard_LRS for cost efficiency; reserve GRS/RAGRS for workloads where the plan explicitly requires geo-redundant durability.

pattern: 97.5% (389 of 399) of storage accounts reporting properties.accessTier use the Hot tier; only 2.5% use Cool.
implication: Default new blob storage accounts to the Hot access tier unless the workload is explicitly archival or infrequently accessed.

pattern: Key Vaults universally enable soft-delete (172 of 172, 100%), 92.5% (123 of 133) keep softDeleteRetentionInDays at 90, 84.2% (16 of 19) reporting enablePurgeProtection have it True, and 60.3% (105 of 174) leave enableRbacAuthorization False (i.e., still use access policies).
implication: Provision new Key Vaults with soft-delete enabled, a 90-day retention window, and purge protection enabled; default the authorization model to access policies (enableRbacAuthorization=false) unless the workload calls for RBAC.

pattern: Network ACLs are permissive by convention: properties.networkAcls.defaultAction is Allow on 93.0% (634 of 682) of reporting resources and properties.networkAcls.bypass is set to AzureServices on 86.3% (474 of 549).
implication: Default networkAcls.defaultAction to Allow and bypass to AzureServices for new resources; only flip to Deny when the workload explicitly requires network isolation.

pattern: 76.6% (209 of 273) of resources reporting properties.disableLocalAuth still allow local auth (False), and 63.7% (158 of 248) of storage accounts have defaultToOAuthAuthentication=False.
implication: Do not disable local/key-based auth by default on new resources; leave disableLocalAuth=false and defaultToOAuthAuthentication=false unless the plan explicitly requires Entra-only auth.

pattern: Zone redundancy is the exception: properties.zoneRedundant is False on 74.5% (243 of 326) of resources reporting it, and properties.zoneRedundancy is Disabled on 95.0% (151 of 159) of resources reporting it.
implication: Default new resources to single-zone (zone-redundancy disabled) for cost efficiency; only enable zone redundancy when the workload explicitly calls for higher availability.

pattern: Container registries follow a consistent hardening posture: properties.adminUserEnabled is False on 76.9% (70 of 91), properties.policies.quarantinePolicy.status is disabled on 100% (91 of 91), properties.networkRuleBypassOptions is AzureServices on 100%, and properties.dataEndpointEnabled is False on 97.8%.
implication: Provision new Azure Container Registries with the admin user disabled, quarantine policy disabled, dedicated data endpoints disabled, and the AzureServices network bypass enabled.

pattern: Compute SKU usage is dominated by the Standard tier (763 of 1,221 sku.tier reports, 62.5%), followed by Regional (15.6%) and Basic (11.8%); Premium tiers together account for under 6%.
implication: Default new SKU selections to the Standard tier for general-purpose workloads; reserve Premium SKUs for resources whose workload description specifically requires premium performance or features.
